column1: # partitions.csv — ESP32-C6 Custom Partition Table
# Target hardware : Arduino Nesso N1 (ESP32-C6
#                   SparkFun ESP32-C6 Qwiic Pocket (4 MB flash)
# Flash mapped    : First 4 MB (0x000000 – 0x3FFFFF)
#                   On the 16 MB Nesso N1 the remaining 12 MB is unused by
#                   this table — no partitions are defined beyond 0x3FFFFF.
# OTA capable     : Yes — two equal-sized app slots (app0 / app1)
# Zigbee capable  : Yes — three dedicated Zigbee stack partitions
# FULL FLASH MAP  (including regions not defined in this table)
#   Address range        Size    Name / role
#   0x000000 – 0x007FFF   32 KB  Bootloader  [NOT in table — hardcoded by IDF]
#   0x008000 – 0x008FFF    4 KB  Partition table itself  [NOT in table]
#   0x009000 – 0x00DFFF   20 KB  nvs          (NVS / Preferences)
#   0x00E000 – 0x00FFFF    8 KB  otadata      (active OTA slot selector)
#   0x010000 – 0x1BFFFF 1728 KB  app0         (OTA slot 0 — factory / primary)
#   0x1C0000 – 0x36FFFF 1728 KB  app1         (OTA slot 1 — OTA target)
#   0x370000 – 0x373FFF   16 KB  zb_storage   (Zigbee network data)
#   0x374000 – 0x374FFF    4 KB  zb_fct       (Zigbee factory config)
#   0x375000 – 0x378FFF   16 KB  zb_nvram     (Zigbee NVRAM / binding tables)
#   0x379000 – 0x3F4FFF  496 KB  spiffs       (SPIFFS filesystem)
#   0x3F5000 – 0x3FEFFF   40 KB  coredump     (crash dump storage)
#   0x3FF000 – 0x3FFFFF    4 KB  phy_init     (RF / PHY calibration data)
#   TOTAL (partitions only)      4060 KB  ← + 36 KB bootloader/table = 4096 KB
#                                           = 4 MB  (entire first bank)
# OTA SLOT SIZING NOTE
# Both app slots must be identical in size. 1728 KB (0x1B0000) was chosen to
# leave room for the Zigbee
# keeping everything within the first 4 MB. The compiled sketch must be
# smaller than 1728 KB for OTA updates to succeed; Arduino IDE shows binary
# size after compile — check it against this limit.
# After a successful OTA upload

# ZIGBEE PARTITION NOTE
# The ESP32-C6 Zigbee stack (esp-zigbee-sdk) requires exactly three
# FAT-typed partitions with these exact names. Renaming or removing any of
# them breaks Zigbee initialisation at runtime:
#   zb_storage  — Primary Zigbee NVS: network key
#                 address table
#                 must survive reboots. Zigbee.factoryReset() wipes this.
#   zb_fct      — Factory configuration written once at first join and used
#                 as the baseline for factory-reset restoration.
#   zb_nvram    — Secondary Zigbee NVRAM: binding table
#                 additional stack state. Also wiped by Zigbee.factoryReset().
# All three use SubType "fat" so the Zigbee stack can use the FAT layer
# directly without going through the main NVS key-value store.
# Column reference
#   Name    — Partition label (max 16 chars); used by esp_partition_find()
#   Type    — "app" (executable) or "data" (everything else)
#   SubType — Qualifies Type; see esp-idf docs for full list
#   Offset  — Byte address in flash; must be 4 KB-aligned (0x1000)
#             App partitions must additionally be 64 KB-aligned (0x10000)
#   Size    — Partition size in bytes; must be a multiple of 4 KB (0x1000)
#   Flags   — Optional; "encrypted" to enable flash encryption for this slot
# Name
# Standard ESP-IDF NVS (Non-Volatile Storage) partition.
# Used by the Arduino Preferences library to persist settings across reboots:
# OTA mode flag
# 20 KB is the IDF-recommended minimum; larger is needed only if many
# Preferences namespaces are in use simultaneously.
nvs
# OTA data partition — mandatory when using two OTA app slots.
# The IDF bootloader reads this 8 KB region to decide which app slot
# (app0 or app1) contains the currently valid firmware image. On a clean
# flash it is blank and the bootloader defaults to app0. After a successful
# OTA upload the Zigbee-side "zigbee-mode" reboot or ElegantOTA library
# updates this partition to point to the newly written slot.
# Must be exactly 8 KB (0x2000) and must sit at 0xE000 — both are IDF
# requirements.
otadata
# Two equal-sized OTA application slots — both must be the same size.
# The bootloader selects the active slot based on "otadata" above.
# app0 (OTA slot 0) — 1728 KB (0x1B0000)
# Factory / primary slot. This is where the sketch is written on first
# flash via USB. Must start at a 64 KB boundary (0x10000 is standard).
app0
# app1 (OTA slot 1) — 1728 KB (0x1B0000)
# OTA target slot. ElegantOTA writes the incoming firmware here while
# app0 continues running. Identical size to app0 is required; the IDF
# will reject an OTA image that is larger than the target slot.
app1
# Three FAT partitions required by the ESP32-C6 Zigbee stack.
# Names
column3: and subtypes are fixed — the stack locates them by name.

column1: # See ZIGBEE PARTITION NOTE in the header above for full details.
# zb_storage — 16 KB: primary Zigbee network state (NWK key
# Wiped by Zigbee.factoryReset() to force a clean re-pair with the hub.
zb_storage
# zb_fct — 4 KB: Zigbee factory / calibration configuration (written once).
zb_fct
# zb_nvram — 16 KB: Zigbee binding table
# Also wiped by Zigbee.factoryReset().
zb_nvram
# SPIFFS (SPI Flash File System) — 496 KB (0x7C000).
# Used by ElegantOTA and the web control panel (WiFi-only / OTA modes) to
# serve static assets. Also available for user configuration files or logs.
# SPIFFS has no subdirectory support; filenames are flat paths up to 32 chars.
# Alternative: replace with "LittleFS" (same SubType spelling in IDF >= 5.x).
spiffs
# Core dump partition — 40 KB (0xA000).
# When a crash occurs and core dump to flash is enabled in sdkconfig
# exception handler serialises CPU registers
# here. Retrieve and decode post-mortem with:
#   idf.py coredump-info  (requires ELF with debug symbols)
# 40 KB is sufficient for single-core ESP32-C6 with a modest stack depth.
# This partition is never written during normal operation.
coredump
# PHY init data — 4 KB (0x1000) at the very top of the 4 MB region.
# Contains RF calibration tables and PHY configuration used by the
# radio subsystem at boot. Written by the factory test process; the IDF
# reads it before initialising WiFi or the 802.15.4 (Zigbee) radio.
# 0x3FF000 is the standard IDF location — do not move this partition.
phy_init
